attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who commits to multi-day efforts. Controls group dynamics through pre-emptive agreements before accelerating for KOM points or stage wins.
team_tactics: Operates with significant autonomy in breakaways for ProTeam squads (Androni). Limited team support visible; relies on individual tactical acumen and negotiation within breaks.
terrain_preference: KOM-classified climbs where points are contested. Thrives in breakaway scenarios requiring sustained effort across consecutive stages rather than pure summit finishes.
vulnerability: Insufficient data on bunch sprint scenarios or when faced with superior climbers in reduced groups. Recovery demands of consecutive breakaway days may create later-race vulnerability.
key_patterns: Consecutive breakaway days: demonstrated three straight days in 2018 Tirreno with KOM control.
Pre-negotiates breakaway dynamics: forced agreement with MOSCA before accelerating for points.
Opportunistic sprint wins from breaks: capitalized on TV sprint in 2020 Tirreno breakaway.
KOM jersey focus: dominant point collection across multiple classified climbs within single race.
form_trajectory: neutral: 2020 win shows continued breakaway effectiveness, but two-year observation gap limits confidence in current trajectory assessment
